content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES Cor4i1ISSIoN
P.O. Box 807
. 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660
. Fax (916) 322-0886
March 17, 1999
Dorothea J. Moore
144 West 27th Street
Merced, California 95340
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. 1-99-051
Dear Ms. Moore:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’ Since your request does not pertain to a specific governmental
decision, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.
2 Please note that this letter
should not be construed to evaluate any conduct that has already taken place. (Section
1 8329(b)(8)(A).)
QUESTION
Does the Act restrict you from managing a project relating to the city hail building?
CONCLUSION
As a former member ofboth the city council and the redevelopment commission, the
revolving door provisions of the Act do not apply to you.
FACTS
On September 21, 1998, you resigned from the Merced City Council and the
Redevelopment Commission. You accepted a position as a consultant for A.T.D. Micro, Inc., for
the period ofNovember 1-30, 1998, until a formal contract could be developed. As of
1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation I 8329(c)(3).)


File No. 1-99-051
Page 2
December 1, 1998, you became a full-time employee for ATD.
3 Your duties include managing a
project partially funded by the redevelopment commission.
ANALYSIS
The Act’s “revolving door” provisions, which prohibit a former public official from
taking part in certain proceedings in which the official participated while in government service
and which prohibit a former public official from appearing before or communicating with his or
her former agency for one year, apply only to “state administrative officials” or to designated
employees of “state administrative agencies.” (Sections 87400-87406.)
In your capacity as a city council member and a member ofthe redevelopment
commission, you were a public official oftwo local governmental agencies. Therefore, the
revolving door provisions of the Act do not apply to you. However, you may wish to consult
with the City of Merced to ascertain if the city has a local ordinance regarding revolving door
provisions for former employees.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Lynda Doherty
Political Reform Consultant, Legal Division
SGC:LD:tls
Other facts included in your request for advice relate to past conduct. We do not provide advice
regarding past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).)
I:\Advice Ltrs\9905 1 .wpd

CONCLUSION: As a former member ofboth the city council and the redevelopment commission, the
revolving door provisions of the Act do not apply to you.
